title: RNA and DNA extraction from frozen tissue for nanopore sequencing
author: Eric RA Pederson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bj7tkrnn
materials: Materials to take to the basement -80° freezer; - gloves - white warm gloves - dry ice - blades - wipes - forceps - ethanol spray - petri-dishes (tissue type written on the lid) - marker - paper tape - normal ice - tubes with Beads, PBS and HALT Protease and Phosphatase Inhibitor Cocktail Abcam Catalog #78440 on ice
Spray with ETOH and then RNaseZap® Thermo Scientific Catalog #AM9780 in the fume hood
Make 3 1.5 ml eppendorf tubes for each sample; 1 with sterile water, 1 with 99% ETOH and 1 with RNaseZap® Thermo Scientific Catalog #AM9780

From the Qiagen AllPrep DNA/RNA Mini Kit Qiagen Catalog #80204 Add 1 ml buffer RLT to 10 ul b-mercaptoethanol Each sample requires 600 ul of buffer RLT + b-mercaptoethanol So for example for 6 samples one would need 3.6 ml of the buffer RLT + b-mercaptoethanol mix. Thus, one would need to prepare 4 ml buffer RLT + 40 ul b-mercaptoethanol . b-mercaptoethanol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M3148-25ML

From the Qiagen AllPrep DNA/RNA Mini Kit Qiagen Catalog #80204 Carefully remove the supernatant by pipetting, and transfer it to the AllPrep DNA spin column placed in a 2 ml collection tube (supplied). Close the lid gently, and centrifuge for 30 s at ≥ 8000 x g (≥10,000 rpm).

From the NEBNext Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina NEB Catalog #E7645S (7 ul) Ultra II End prep reaction buffer (NEB, E7647AA) (3 ul) Ultra I End prep Enzyme mix (NEB, E7646AA) (50 ul) cDNA with adapters Total volume of 60 ul
Serapure/Ampure purification using a 1:1 mix ration (60:60)

steps.title: Prepare labelled 2 ml low bind...
steps.instruction: Prepare labelled 2 ml low bind tubes with 4 Zirconium Oxide Beads 2.0 mm RNase Free 4 mL beads in each Zirconium Oxide Beads 2.0 mm RNase Free 4 mL Contributed by users Catalog #ZROB20-RNA

steps.title: Add to the 2
steps.instruction: Add to the 2.0 ml eppidendorf tube that already has the beads ( Zirconium Oxide Beads 2.0 mm RNase Free 4 mL ) with 600 ul RLT buffer and b-mercaptoethanol mixture.

steps.title: In the most sterile way possible,...
steps.instruction: In the most sterile way possible, cut a small piece of tissue, half a pea size or so, and put it into one of the labelled 2ml tubes with Beads and the RLT mixture on ice Make sure to use the ethanol to clean everything and not to cut yourself. Use the forceps to grab the tissue that flies away.

steps.title: Use the bullet blender to destroy...
steps.instruction: Use the bullet blender to destroy the tissue (Tape on lids) - 3 minutes - speed setting 8

steps.title: Get tissue from the -80 freezer...
steps.instruction: Get tissue from the -80 freezer and place on dry ice

steps.title: Centrifuge the lysate for 3 min at maximum speed
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the lysate for 3 min at maximum speed.

steps.title: Label all the spin columns and the collection tubes properly
steps.instruction: Label all the spin columns and the collection tubes properly

steps.title: Place the AllPrep DNA spin column...
steps.instruction: Place the AllPrep DNA spin column in a new 2 ml collection tube (supplied), and store at room temperature (15–25oC) or at 4oC for later DNA purification in steps 14–17. Use the flow-through for RNA purification in the following steps

steps.title: Add 1 volume (usually 600 μl)...
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume (usually 600 μl) of 70% ethanol to the flow- through from step 5, and mix well by pipetting. Do not centrifuge.

steps.title: Transfer up to 700 μl of...
steps.instruction: Transfer up to 700 μl of the sample, including any precipitate that may have formed, to an RNeasy spin column placed in a 2 ml collection tube (supplied). Close the lid gently, and centrifuge for 15 s at ≥ 8000 x g (≥ 10,000 rpm). Discard the flowthrough.

steps.title: Add 350 μl Buffer RW1 to...
steps.instruction: Add 350 μl Buffer RW1 to the RNeasy spin column, and centrifuge for 15 s at ≥8000 x g (≥10,000 rpm) to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the flow-through.* Reuse the collection tube in step E4.

steps.title: Add 500 μl Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column. Close the lid gently, and centrifuge for 15 s at ≥ 8000 x g (≥ 10,000 rpm) to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 500 μl Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column. Close the lid gently, and centrifuge for 2 min at ≥ 8000 x g (≥ 10,000 rpm) to wash the spin column membrane

steps.title: Place the RNeasy spin column in...
steps.instruction: Place the RNeasy spin column in a new 2 ml collection tube (supplied), and discard the old collection tube with the flow- through. Centrifuge at full speed for 1 min.

steps.title: Place the RNeasy spin column in a new 1
steps.instruction: Place the RNeasy spin column in a new 1.5 ml collection tube (supplied). Add 30 μl RNase-free water directly to the spin column membrane. Close the lid gently, and centrifuge for 1 min at ≥ 8000 x g (≥10,000 rpm) to elute the RNA.

steps.title: If the expected RNA yield is...
steps.instruction: If the expected RNA yield is > 30 μg, repeat the previous step using another 30 μl of RNase-free water, or using the eluate from step 12 (if high RNA concentration is required). Reuse the collection tube from the previous step.

steps.title: Run the tapestation and the nanodrop on the RNA
steps.instruction: Run the tapestation and the nanodrop on the RNA.

steps.title: Store the RNA in the -80...
steps.instruction: Store the RNA in the -80 freezer for long term storage, or leave on ice until after the DNA extractions to preform the cDNA synthesis

steps.title: Add 500 μl Buffer AW1 to...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl Buffer AW1 to the AllPrep DNA spin column from before the RNA extraction. Close the lid gently, and centrifuge for 15 s at ≥ 8000 x g (10,000 rpm). Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 500 μl Buffer AW2 to...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl Buffer AW2 to the AllPrep DNA spin column. Close the lid gently, and centrifuge for 2 min at full speed to wash the spin column membrane.

steps.title: Buffer AW1 is supplied as a concentrate
steps.instruction: Buffer AW1 is supplied as a concentrate. Ensure that ethanol is added to Buffer AW1 before use Buffer AW2 is supplied as a concentrate. Ensure that ethanol is added to Buffer AW2 before use

steps.title: Place the AllPrep DNA spin column in a new 1
steps.instruction: Place the AllPrep DNA spin column in a new 1.5 ml collection tube (supplied). Add 100 μl Buffer EB directly to the spin column membrane and close the lid. Incubate at room temperature (15–25oC) for 1 min, and then centrifuge for 1 min at ≥ 8000 x g (10,000 rpm) to elute the DNA.

steps.title: Repeat previous step to elute further DNA
steps.instruction: Repeat previous step to elute further DNA.

steps.title: Run the tapestation (genomic) and the Qubit on the DNA
steps.instruction: Run the tapestation (genomic) and the Qubit on the DNA.

steps.title: Store the DNA in the -20°...
steps.instruction: Store the DNA in the -20° or -80° freezer for long term storage.

steps.title: From the cDNA-PCR Sequencing (SQK-PCS109) Oxford...
steps.instruction: From the cDNA-PCR Sequencing (SQK-PCS109) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-PCS109 Prepare the following reaction in a 0.2 ml PCR tube

steps.title: Mix gently by flicking the tube and spin down
steps.instruction: Mix gently by flicking the tube and spin down

steps.title: (X ul) 1 ng PolyA+ RNA...
steps.instruction: (X ul) 1 ng PolyA+ RNA (or ~50 ng total RNA) (1 ul) VN primers (VNP), at 2 uM (1 ul) 10 mM dNTPs (9-X ul) RNase-free water Total volume of 11 ul

steps.title: Incubate at 65° for 5 minutes...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 65° for 5 minutes and then snap cool on a pre-chilled freezer block

steps.title: In a separate 0
steps.instruction: In a separate 0.2 ml PCR tube, mix the following reaction

steps.title: Mix gently by flicking the tube and spin down
steps.instruction: Mix gently by flicking the tube and spin down

steps.title: (4 ul) 5x RT buffer (1...
steps.instruction: (4 ul) 5x RT buffer (1 ul) RNase Out (1 ul) Nuclease-free water (2 ul) Strand-Switching Primer (SSP, at 10 uM) Total vollume of 8 ul

steps.title: Add the strand-switching buffer to the...
steps.instruction: Add the strand-switching buffer to the snap-cooled, annealed mRNA and mix by flicking the tube and spin down

steps.title: Incubate at 42° for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at 42° for 2 minutes

steps.title: Add 1 ul of Maxima H minus Reverse Transciptase
steps.instruction: Add 1 ul of Maxima H minus Reverse Transciptase. The total volume should now be 20 ul

steps.title: Mix by flicking the tube and spin down
steps.instruction: Mix by flicking the tube and spin down

steps.title: Incubate using the following protocol
steps.instruction: Incubate using the following protocol

steps.title: Add adaptors to the cDNA using PCR
steps.instruction: Add adaptors to the cDNA using PCR

steps.title: From the Prime STAR GXL DNA polymerase Takara Bio Inc
steps.instruction: From the Prime STAR GXL DNA polymerase Takara Bio Inc. Catalog #R050B And PCR barcoding expansion 96 Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #EXP-PBC096 Reaction mix; (10 ul) 5x Prime STAR GXL buffer (1 ul) 10 mM KAPA dNTP mix (1 ul) Prime STAR GXL DNA polymerase (5 ul) cDNA (1 ul) primer (Barcode mix from nanopore

steps.title: PCR program 1 cycle 98° -...
steps.instruction: PCR program 1 cycle 98° - 30 seconds 10 cycles 98° - 10 seconds 64° - 15 seconds 68° - 10 seconds 1 cycle 68° - 5 minutes 4° - ∞

steps.title: Bead extraction
steps.instruction: Bead extraction: - Add 1.8X beads to samples - flick to mix - incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature - use the magnet to collect the DNA-beads - discard supernatant - wash with 70% ethanol - dry at 37° for 3 minutes - elute with 20 ul TE buffer - incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature - use the magnet to collect the DNA-beads - collect supernatant into a new tube

steps.title: Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature

steps.title: From the T4 RNA Ligase 2...
steps.instruction: From the T4 RNA Ligase 2 - 750 units New England Biolabs Catalog #M0239L (60 ul) purified DNA (25 ul) Ligation buffer (10 ul) Ligase (5 ul) adaptor mix

steps.title: Run Qubit
steps.instruction: Run Qubit

steps.title: From the Flow Cell Priming Kit...
steps.instruction: From the Flow Cell Priming Kit Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #EXP-FLP002 And using a Flow Cell (R9) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #FLO-MIN106D DNA library - 12 ul ( of the 14 ul) Sequencing buffer (SQB) - 37.5 ul Loading Beads - 25.5 ul Total volume of 75 ul

steps.title: PCR program 20° for 5 minutes 65° for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: PCR program 20° for 5 minutes 65° for 5 minutes

steps.title: Bead extraction
steps.instruction: Bead extraction: - Add 1.8X beads to samples - flick to mix - incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature - use the magnet to collect the DNA-beads - discard supernatant - wash with 70% ethanol - dry at 37° for 3 minutes - elute with 20 ul TE buffer - incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature - use the magnet to collect the DNA-beads - collect supernatant into a new tube

steps.title: Thaw the Sequencing Buffer (SQB), Loading...
steps.instruction: Thaw the Sequencing Buffer (SQB), Loading Beads (LB), Flush Tether (FLT) and one tube of Flush Buffer (FB) at RT before placing the tubes on ice as soon as thawing is complete

steps.title: - Add 1
steps.instruction: - Add 1.8X beads to samples - flick to mix - incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature - use the magnet to collect the DNA-beads - discard supernatant - wash with SFB (250 ul) - dry at 37° for 3 minutes - elute with 15 ul TE buffer - incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature - use the magnet to collect the DNA-beads - collect supernatant into a new tube

steps.title: From the DNase I (RNase-free) -...
steps.instruction: From the DNase I (RNase-free) - 1,000 units New England Biolabs Catalog #M0303S Make DNase solution.

steps.title: Prepare DNase I stock solution before...
steps.instruction: Prepare DNase I stock solution before using the RNase-Free DNase Set for the first time. Dissolve the solid DNase I (1500 Kunitz units) in 550 μl of the RNase-free water provided. To avoid loss of DNase I, do not open the vial. Inject RNase-free water into the vial using an RNase-free needle and syringe. Mix gently by inverting the vial. Do not vortex.

steps.title: For long-term storage of DNase I,...
steps.instruction: For long-term storage of DNase I, remove the stock solution from the glass vial, divide it into single-use aliquots, and store at –20oC for up to 9 months. Thawed aliquots can be stored at 2–8oC for up to 6 weeks. Do not refreeze the aliquots after thawing.

steps.title: Add 10 μl DNase I stock...
steps.instruction: Add 10 μl DNase I stock solution (see above) to 70 μl Buffer RDD. Mix by gently inverting the tube, and centrifuge briefly to collect residual liquid from the sides of the tube. Buffer RDD is supplied with the RNase-Free DNase Set. Note: DNase I is especially sensitive to physical denaturation. Mixing should only be carried out by gently inverting the tube. Do not vortex.

steps.title: Add the DNase I incubation mix...
steps.instruction: Add the DNase I incubation mix (80 μl) directly to the RNeasy spin column membrane, and incubate at room temperature (20–30oC) for 15 min. Note: Be sure to add the DNase I incubation mix directly to the RNeasy spin column membrane. DNase digestion will be incomplete if part of the mix sticks to the walls or the O-ring of the spin column.

steps.title: Add 350 μl Buffer RW1 to...
steps.instruction: Add 350 μl Buffer RW1 to the RNeasy spin column, and centrifuge for 15 s at ≥8000 x g (≥10,000 rpm). Discard the flow-through. Continue with step 9 (22) of the protocol on page 26 (i.e., the first wash with Buffer RPE). Reuse the collection tube in step 9 (22).

steps.title: Turn on centrfuge to 4°
steps.instruction: Turn on centrfuge to 4°.

steps.title: Mix the Sequencing Buffer (SQB) and...
steps.instruction: Mix the Sequencing Buffer (SQB) and Flush Buffer (FB) tubes by vortexing, spin down and return to ice.

steps.title: Spin down the Flush Tether (FLT)...
steps.instruction: Spin down the Flush Tether (FLT) tube, mix by pipetting, and return to ice.

steps.title: Open the lid of the nanopore...
steps.instruction: Open the lid of the nanopore sequencing device and slide the flow cell’s priming port cover clockwise so that the priming port is visible.

steps.title: After opening the priming port, check...
steps.instruction: After opening the priming port, check for small bubbles under the cover. Draw back a small volume to remove any bubble (a few µl) as follows:

steps.title: Prepare the flow cell priming mix as follows:
steps.instruction: Prepare the flow cell priming mix as follows:

steps.title: Set a P1000 pipette to 200 µl
steps.instruction: Set a P1000 pipette to 200 µl

steps.title: Turn the wheel until the dial...
steps.instruction: Turn the wheel until the dial shows 220-230 µl, or until you can see a small volume of buffer entering the pipette tip Care must be taken when drawing back buffer from the flow cell. The array of pores must be covered by buffer at all times. Removing more than 20-30 µl risks a loss of sequencing channels.

steps.title: Insert the tip into the priming port
steps.instruction: Insert the tip into the priming port

steps.title: Care must be taken when drawing...
steps.instruction: Care must be taken when drawing back buffer from the flow cell. The array of pores must be covered by buffer at all times. Removing more than 20-30 µl risks a loss of sequencing channels.

steps.title: Add 30 µl of thawed and...
steps.instruction: Add 30 µl of thawed and mixed Flush Tether (FLT) directly to the tube of thawed and mixed Flush Buffer (FB), and mix by pipetting up and down.

steps.title: Load 800 µl of the priming...
steps.instruction: Load 800 µl of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port, avoiding the introduction of air bubbles. Wait for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Thoroughly mix the contents of the...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix the contents of the Loading Beads (LB) by pipetting. Note that The Loading Beads (LB) tube contains a suspension of beads. These beads settle very quickly. It is vital that they are mixed immediately before use.

steps.title: In a new tube, prepare the...
steps.instruction: In a new tube, prepare the library for loading as follows:
37.5 µl Sequencing Buffer (SQB) 25.5 µl Loading Beads (LB), mixed immediately before use 12 µl cDNA library prepared as described in SOP ID:0005 75 ul Total

steps.title: Gently lift the SpotON sample port...
steps.instruction: Gently lift the SpotON sample port cover to make the SpotON sample port accessible. Load 200 µl of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port (not the SpotON sample port), avoiding the introduction of air bubbles.

steps.title: Mix the prepared library gently by...
steps.instruction: Mix the prepared library gently by pipetting up and down just prior to loading.

steps.title: Add 75 μl of sample to...
steps.instruction: Add 75 μl of sample to the flow cell via the SpotON sample port in a dropwise fashion. Ensure each drop flows into the port before adding the next.

steps.title: Gently replace the SpotON sample port...
steps.instruction: Gently replace the SpotON sample port cover, making sure the bung enters the SpotON port, close the priming port and replace the MinION lid.
